column_descriptions.flankTarget: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped within the fragment length from the target. (fragment length is usually defined as a the median read length)
column_descriptions.offTarget_HighCov: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped completely off-target but accumulate with high coverage (usually more than 19 reads but this depends on the application) in specific places in the genome. This could happen for example when pseudogenes are present in off-target regions. A high number of off-target alignments result in lower on-target coverage.
column_descriptions.offTarget_LowCov: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped completely off-target that are scattered across the genome with low coverage (usually less than 20 reads but this depends on the application). This could happen for example if problems with library preparation occur. A high number of off-target alignments result in lower on-target coverage.
column_descriptions.onTarget: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped on-target

table_summary: This table shows what fraction of reads were mapped on-target next to the target and off-target. A high number of off-target reads can indicate an issue. A high number of off-target reads will decrease the power of the variant calling.
title: Run - ontarget-mapping-statistics-table-HCS

column_descriptions.flankTarget: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped within the fragment length from the target. (fragment length is usually defined as a the median read length)
column_descriptions.offTarget_HighCov: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped completely off-target but accumulate with high coverage (usually more than 19 reads but this depends on the application) in specific places in the genome. This could happen for example when pseudogenes are present in off-target regions. A high number of off-target alignments result in lower on-target coverage.
column_descriptions.offTarget_LowCov: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped completely off-target that are scattered across the genome with low coverage (usually less than 20 reads but this depends on the application). This could happen for example if problems with library preparation occur. A high number of off-target alignments result in lower on-target coverage.
column_descriptions.onTarget: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped on-target

table_summary: This table shows what fraction of reads were mapped on-target next to the target and off-target. A high number of off-target reads can indicate an issue. A high number of off-target reads will decrease the power of the variant calling.
title: Run - ontarget-mapping-statistics-table-HCS

column_descriptions.11-100: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found between eleven to one hundred times.
column_descriptions.2: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found twice.
column_descriptions.3: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found three times.
column_descriptions.4: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found four times.
column_descriptions.5: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found five times.
column_descriptions.6-10: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found between six to ten times.
column_descriptions.>100: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found more than a hundred times.
column_descriptions.duplicate fraction: The fraction of mapped paired-reads that are duplicates of an original read.

table_summary: PCR duplicates make the coverage seem higher than it actually is and give the illusion of high statistical confidence. They can change the variant fractions and result in calling variants that are not actually present or vice-versa. Additionally if an error appears at the beginning of the amplification the same error will be propagated to many reads. This table contains the PCR duplicate rate per sample as well as counts of reads that have 2-5 6-10 and over 100 duplicates.
title: Run - pcr-duplicates-table-HCS

column_descriptions.11-100: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found between eleven to one hundred times.
column_descriptions.2: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found twice.
column_descriptions.3: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found three times.
column_descriptions.4: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found four times.
column_descriptions.5: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found five times.
column_descriptions.6-10: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found between six to ten times.
column_descriptions.>100: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found more than a hundred times.
column_descriptions.duplicate fraction: The fraction of mapped paired-reads that are duplicates of an original read.

They can change the variant fractions and result in calling variants that are not actually present or vice-versa. title: Run - pcr-duplicates-table-HCS

column_descriptions.Number of Mapped reads: This is the number of reads that were successfully mapped to the reference genome
column_descriptions.Number of reads: This is the number of all the reads that were used as input for the alignment process
column_descriptions.Number of reads with problematic mate: This is the number of reads that had a problematic mate. This number is computed as [the number of mapped reads - 2*number of mapped pairs]
column_descriptions.Percentage of Mapped reads: This is the percentage of reads that were successfully mapped to the reference genome. The percentage of mapped reads is a good metrics to check the quality of the mapping process overall sequencing accuracy and the presence of contaminating DNA

table_summary: This table contains the sample id the number of reads number of reads with a problematic mate and the percentage of mapped reads. The percentage of mapped reads is a good metric to check the quality of the mapping process overall sequencing accuracy and the presence of contaminating DNA
title: Run - read-counts-overview-table

column_descriptions.Number of Mapped reads: This is the number of reads that were successfully mapped to the reference genome
column_descriptions.Number of reads: This is the number of all the reads that were used as input for the alignment process
column_descriptions.Number of reads with problematic mate: This is the number of reads that had a problematic mate. This number is computed as [the number of mapped reads - 2*number of mapped pairs]
column_descriptions.Percentage of Mapped reads: This is the percentage of reads that were successfully mapped to the reference genome. The percentage of mapped reads is a good metrics to check the quality of the mapping process overall sequencing accuracy and the presence of contaminating DNA

table_summary: This table contains the sample id the number of reads number of reads with a problematic mate and the percentage of mapped reads. The percentage of mapped reads is a good metric to check the quality of the mapping process overall sequencing accuracy and the presence of contaminating DNA
title: Run - read-counts-overview-table

column_descriptions.End soft clip: This is the number of reads that were mapped after being soft-clipped at the end. (percentage in parenthesis)
column_descriptions.Front soft clip: This is the number of reads that were mapped after being soft-clipped at the front of the read. (percentage in parenthesis)
column_descriptions.No soft clip: This is the number of reads that were mapped without soft-clipping. (percentage in parenthesis)
column_descriptions.Soft clip both ends: This is the number of reads that were mapped after being soft-clipped on both sides. (percentage in parenthesis)

table_summary: If a read cannot be aligned over its full length during the alignment process some bases will be 'soft-clipped' at the borders of the read. This means that they will be ignored for the alignment and the part that is not soft-clipped can therefore be alignment properly to a place in the reference genome. This procedure caries less penalty than mismatching bases in the alignment process. Depending on the data there might be a lot of read alignments with soft-clipping however the number of bases that are clipped should be small. If for a lot of reads the number of soft-clipped bases is high this indicates that there was a problem and that a lot of the data are discarded.
title: Run - softclip-percentage-table

column_descriptions.End soft clip: This is the number of reads that were mapped after being soft-clipped at the end. (percentage in parenthesis)
column_descriptions.Front soft clip: This is the number of reads that were mapped after being soft-clipped at the front of the read. (percentage in parenthesis)
column_descriptions.No soft clip: This is the number of reads that were mapped without soft-clipping. (percentage in parenthesis)
column_descriptions.Soft clip both ends: This is the number of reads that were mapped after being soft-clipped on both sides. (percentage in parenthesis)

title: Run - softclip-percentage-table

column_descriptions.1000x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 1000x or more.
column_descriptions.100x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 100x or more.
column_descriptions.200x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 200x or more.
column_descriptions.25x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 25x or more.
column_descriptions.500x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 500x or more.
column_descriptions.50x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 50x or more.
column_descriptions.Coverage 10% quantile: This is the 10% quantile of the coverage distribution. If this number is x it means that 10% of the regions have coverage lower than x and 90% have coverage higher than x.
column_descriptions.Coverage Heterogeneity: It is important that all positions in the target regions have similar coverage. Coverage heterogeneity is calculated as the percentage of base pairs (in the target region) whose coverage is lower than 0.2 of the median target coverage or higher than 5x the median target coverage. The lower this number the better.

table_summary: An important prerequisite for reliable variant detection from next-generation sequencing data is the coverage of the target regions. The base coverage is defined as the number of reads covering a base. Our tool counts regions of several base pairs with coverage lower than a certain threshold set according to the application. This table shows the number of low coverage regions per sample were detected in the data.
title: Run - target-region-coverage-table-HCS

column_descriptions.6-10: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found between six to ten times.
column_descriptions.>100: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found more than a hundred times.
column_descriptions.duplicate fraction: The fraction of mapped paired-reads that are duplicates of an original read.

table_summary: PCR duplicates make the coverage seem higher than it actually is and give the illusion of high statistical confidence. They can change the variant fractions and result in calling variants that are not actually present or vice-versa. Additionally if an error appears at the beginning of the amplification the same error will be propagated to many reads. This table contains the PCR duplicate rate per sample as well as counts of reads that have 2-5 6-10 and over 100 duplicates.
title: SG061-LPA - pcr-duplicates-table-HCS

column_descriptions.Number of Mapped reads: This is the number of reads that were successfully mapped to the reference genome
column_descriptions.Number of reads: This is the number of all the reads that were used as input for the alignment process
column_descriptions.Number of reads with problematic mate: This is the number of reads that had a problematic mate. This number is computed as [the number of mapped reads - 2*number of mapped pairs]
column_descriptions.Percentage of Mapped reads: This is the percentage of reads that were successfully mapped to the reference genome. The percentage of mapped reads is a good metrics to check the quality of the mapping process overall sequencing accuracy and the presence of contaminating DNA

table_summary: This table contains the sample id the number of reads number of reads with a problematic mate and the percentage of mapped reads. The percentage of mapped reads is a good metric to check the quality of the mapping process overall sequencing accuracy and the presence of contaminating DNA
title: SG061-LPA - read-counts-overview-table

column_descriptions.End soft clip: This is the number of reads that were mapped after being soft-clipped at the end. (percentage in parenthesis)
column_descriptions.Front soft clip: This is the number of reads that were mapped after being soft-clipped at the front of the read. (percentage in parenthesis)
column_descriptions.No soft clip: This is the number of reads that were mapped without soft-clipping. (percentage in parenthesis)
column_descriptions.Soft clip both ends: This is the number of reads that were mapped after being soft-clipped on both sides. (percentage in parenthesis)

table_summary: If a read cannot be aligned over its full length during the alignment process some bases will be 'soft-clipped' at the borders of the read. This means that they will be ignored for the alignment and the part that is not soft-clipped can therefore be alignment properly to a place in the reference genome. This procedure caries less penalty than mismatching bases in the alignment process. Depending on the data there might be a lot of read alignments with soft-clipping however the number of bases that are clipped should be small. If for a lot of reads the number of soft-clipped bases is high this indicates that there was a problem and that a lot of the data are discarded.
title: SG061-LPA - softclip-percentage-table

column_descriptions.1000x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 1000x or more.
column_descriptions.100x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 100x or more.
column_descriptions.200x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 200x or more.
column_descriptions.25x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 25x or more.
column_descriptions.500x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 500x or more.
column_descriptions.50x: Percentage of basepairs within the target region with coverage 50x or more.
column_descriptions.Coverage 10% quantile: This is the 10% quantile of the coverage distribution. If this number is x it means that 10% of the regions have coverage lower than x and 90% have coverage higher than x.
column_descriptions.Coverage Heterogeneity: It is important that all positions in the target regions have similar coverage. Coverage heterogeneity is calculated as the percentage of base pairs (in the target region) whose coverage is lower than 0.2 of the median target coverage or higher than 5x the median target coverage. The lower this number the better.

table_summary: An important prerequisite for reliable variant detection from next-generation sequencing data is the coverage of the target regions. The base coverage is defined as the number of reads covering a base. Our tool counts regions of several base pairs with coverage lower than a certain threshold set according to the application. This table shows the number of low coverage regions per sample were detected in the data.
title: SG061-LPA - target-region-coverage-table-HCS

column_descriptions.flankTarget: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped within the fragment length from the target. (fragment length is usually defined as a the median read length)
column_descriptions.offTarget_HighCov: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped completely off-target but accumulate with high coverage (usually more than 19 reads but this depends on the application) in specific places in the genome. This could happen for example when pseudogenes are present in off-target regions. A high number of off-target alignments result in lower on-target coverage.
column_descriptions.offTarget_LowCov: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped completely off-target that are scattered across the genome with low coverage (usually less than 20 reads but this depends on the application). This could happen for example if problems with library preparation occur. A high number of off-target alignments result in lower on-target coverage.
column_descriptions.onTarget: Percentage of mapped base pairs that were mapped on-target

table_summary: This table shows what fraction of reads were mapped on-target next to the target and off-target. A high number of off-target reads can indicate an issue. A high number of off-target reads will decrease the power of the variant calling.
title: SG062-LPA - ontarget-mapping-statistics-table-HCS

column_descriptions.11-100: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found between eleven to one hundred times.
column_descriptions.2: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found twice.
column_descriptions.3: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found three times.
column_descriptions.4: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found four times.
column_descriptions.5: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found five times.
column_descriptions.6-10: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found between six to ten times.
column_descriptions.>100: Number of identical mapped paired-reads found more than a hundred times.
column_descriptions.duplicate fraction: The fraction of mapped paired-reads that are duplicates of an original read.
